seoTitle: How to Use Typeform Surveys Effectively
metaDescription: Learn how to create, customize, and analyze surveys in Typeform to collect better feedback and improve response rates.
intro: Getting useful feedback depends on how a survey is built, not just what questions it asks. This guide covers how to create and use Typeform Surveys, from adding question types and logic to analyzing results, so you can gather more reliable insights from your audience.

steps.heading: Create a new survey
steps.body: Start a Typeform survey by choosing a relevant template or building it from scratch based on what you need to learn.

steps.heading: Customize questions and design
steps.body: Add different question types and adjust the theme to keep the survey clear, engaging, and on-brand.

steps.heading: Configure logic and settings
steps.body: Use conditional logic to tailor the survey path based on responses, reducing irrelevant questions for each participant.

steps.heading: Share and analyze results
steps.body: Publish the survey, share it with your audience, and review the collected responses using Typeform's analysis tools.

faq.question: What types of surveys can I build with Typeform?
faq.answer: Typeform supports customer feedback surveys, market research, employee opinion surveys, and general questionnaires.

faq.question: How do I improve survey completion rates?
faq.answer: Using conditional logic to skip irrelevant questions and keeping the design clean and focused can help improve completion rates.

faq.question: Can I analyze survey results directly in Typeform?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform provides response summaries and analytics you can review without needing to export the data first.

faq.question: Is Typeform good for employee feedback surveys?
faq.answer: Yes, it's used by HR teams and organizations for gathering employee opinions in addition to customer-facing surveys.
